findings.summary: generationTransformers.ts uses emoji-prefixed comments (stars, chain links, target) that are hallmarks of LLM-generated code and add no clarity beyond the field name itself.
evidence: Line 433: `starred: genData.starred ?? false, // ⭐ Pass through starred status`
Line 434: `based_on: genData.based_on ?? undefined, // 🔗 Pass through based_on for lineage tracking`
Line 435: `derivedCount: genData.derivedCount ?? 0, // 🔢 Pass through variant count`
findings.suggestion: Remove the emoji comments entirely. The field names are self-documenting.
findings.reasoning: Emoji-decorated restating comments are a classic LLM-generation hallmark. The field assignments are trivially obvious.

findings.summary: useSmartPolling.ts has 6 emoji-prefixed comments (target emoji) that read as LLM annotations rather than genuine developer notes.
evidence: Line 91: `// 🎯 FIX: Stabilize queryKey for dependency comparison`
Line 98: `// 🎯 FIX: Throttle forceUpdate to prevent rapid re-renders`
Line 107: `// 🎯 OPTIMIZATION: Only re-render if the calculated polling interval actually changes`
Line 110: `// 🎯 THROTTLE: Batch rapid updates into a single re-render`
Line 146: `// 🎯 NEW: Respect when freshness manager disables polling completely`
Line 209: `// 🎯 NEW: Default to minInterval: false to allow full polling disable`
findings.suggestion: Strip emoji prefixes. Keep the descriptive text where it adds value (e.g., the stabilize/throttle explanations are useful, just drop the emoji prefix).
findings.reasoning: The target emoji pattern across multiple files is a consistent LLM generation signature. The comments themselves sometimes add value but the emoji prefix is noise.

findings.summary: Two separate useRenderLogger implementations exist: hooks/useRenderLogger.ts (simple, uses logger) and lib/debugRendering.ts (proper, with loop detection). Both are imported by different consumers.
evidence: hooks/useRenderLogger.ts: imported by GenerationsPane.tsx and TasksPane.tsx
lib/debugRendering.ts: imported by useAutoSaveSettings.ts, useImageGenForm.ts, ProjectContext.tsx, useDebouncedSettingsSave.ts, usePersistentToolState.ts, useUserUIState.ts
The hooks version (lines 12-26) uses log() with no loop detection; the lib version (lines 24-53) has proper loop detection with timestamps
findings.suggestion: Delete hooks/useRenderLogger.ts and update GenerationsPane.tsx and TasksPane.tsx to import from lib/debugRendering.ts. The lib version is strictly better (has loop detection).
findings.reasoning: Two implementations of the same hook is confusing and the simpler one lacks the loop detection feature that makes the utility actually useful.

findings.summary: useEditVideoSettings.ts has a module-level docstring and a function-level docstring that say the same thing in slightly different words.
evidence: Lines 1-7: Module docstring says 'Uses useAutoSaveSettings with Edit Video specific defaults'
Lines 17-19: Function docstring says 'Uses useAutoSaveSettings with Edit Video specific defaults'
findings.suggestion: Keep only the function-level docstring (or neither, since the function body is 8 lines and self-documenting).
findings.reasoning: Saying the same thing twice in a 30-line file is classic LLM over-documentation.

findings.summary: shots/mappers.ts has restating comments on obvious field assignments and a module-level docstring that restates the function's purpose.
evidence: Line 72: `id: sg.id, // shot_generations.id - unique per entry in shot`
Line 73: `generation_id: gen.id, // generations.id - the actual generation`
Lines 1-4: Module docstring 'Data mappers for shot-related data transformations. These ensure consistent data shapes across all hooks.' restates the obvious
findings.suggestion: The IMPORTANT comment on line 57-59 is valuable (explains the invariant). Remove the per-field restating comments and the generic module docstring.
findings.reasoning: The field names are clear. The critical comment about all hooks using this mapper is the only one that matters.

findings.summary: useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx has inline JSDoc comments on each return field that restate what the field name already says.
evidence: Line 62: `/** Opens the confirmation dialog for the given generation ID */`
Line 64: `/** Render this component in your JSX tree: `<DeleteConfirmDialog />` */`
Line 66: `/** Whether a delete is currently in progress */`
Line 68: `/** The ID currently being deleted (or null) */`
findings.suggestion: Remove inline return-field JSDoc comments. The function-level docstring and @example are sufficient.
findings.reasoning: Field names `requestDelete`, `isPending`, `deletingId` are self-documenting. The comments add no information.

findings.summary: useSmartPolling.ts SmartPollingConfig and SmartPollingResult interfaces have JSDoc on every single field, most of which restate the field name.
evidence: Line 14-16: `/** The query key this polling config applies to */ queryKey`
Line 41-43: `/** React Query refetchInterval - use this in your useQuery config */ refetchInterval`
Line 47-49: `/** React Query staleTime - use this in your useQuery config */ staleTime`
Line 53-55: `/** Whether data is considered fresh based on realtime events */ isDataFresh`
findings.suggestion: Keep only the non-obvious comments (e.g., the minInterval false behavior). Remove comments that just restate the TypeScript type and name.
findings.reasoning: Every-field JSDoc on internal interfaces is LLM over-documentation. Only the minInterval false-disabling-polling behavior adds information.

findings.summary: useSmartPolling.ts captures `throttleTimeout` on line 104 but only uses it in cleanup, and the variable `debug` appears in the effect deps array (line 128) but is never used inside the effect.
evidence: Line 104: `const throttleTimeout = throttleTimeoutRef.current;` captured at effect start, but throttleTimeoutRef.current may have changed by cleanup time
Line 128: `}, [queryKeyString, debug]);` - debug is in deps but not used in the effect body
findings.suggestion: Remove `debug` from the effect deps (it's only used in the render body, not the effect). The throttleTimeout capture is a potential bug - cleanup should read throttleTimeoutRef.current directly.
findings.reasoning: Stale closure capture of a ref value and unused deps are signs of code that was written by an LLM that added deps defensively without reasoning about what the effect actually needs.

findings.summary: useSmartPolling.ts constructs detailed pollingReason strings with mobile-specific variants that are only used in the debug output object, which most callers never access.
evidence: Lines 149-170: Six different pollingReason strings with mobile/desktop variants
Lines 183-193: pollingReason only used in debug object which requires debug=true
Line 208-214: useSmartPollingConfig (the main export) never passes debug=true
findings.suggestion: Simplify pollingReason to only be computed when debug=true, or remove the mobile-specific string variants since the multiplier value already conveys the information.
findings.reasoning: Computing 6 conditional strings on every render that are discarded 99% of the time is wasteful over-engineering.

findings.summary: PersistedFormSettings interface in ImageGenerationForm/types.ts is defined but never referenced anywhere in the codebase.
evidence: Line 45: `interface PersistedFormSettings {` - not exported, not used in the file
Grep for PersistedFormSettings returns only the definition at types.ts:45
findings.suggestion: Delete the PersistedFormSettings interface.
findings.reasoning: Dead code. It appears to be a leftover from a migration where settings were moved to other patterns.

findings.summary: The `whiteText` prop was deprecated in favor of `darkSurface` but is still the primary prop used by most consumers (MediaGalleryGrid, MediaGalleryHeader, MediaGalleryPagination, skeleton-gallery) and hasn't been migrated.
evidence: MediaTypeFilter.tsx line 15: `/** @deprecated Use darkSurface instead */ whiteText?: boolean;`
ShotFilter.tsx line 23: `/** @deprecated Use darkSurface instead */ whiteText?: boolean;`
MediaGalleryGrid.tsx line 20, 84: still accepts and uses whiteText prop throughout
MediaGalleryHeader.tsx line 18, 73: still accepts and uses whiteText prop throughout
MediaGalleryPagination.tsx line 21, 42: still uses whiteText directly (not even aliased to darkSurface)
GenerationsPane.tsx line 580, 610: passes `whiteText={true}` (the main consumer)
findings.suggestion: Either complete the migration (rename whiteText to darkSurface in all components) or remove the @deprecated annotation. The migration has only been done in MediaTypeFilter and ShotFilter but not propagated to the parent components that still thread whiteText through.
findings.reasoning: A half-finished deprecation is worse than no deprecation. MediaGalleryPagination still uses whiteText directly without even the darkSurface alias, meaning the migration stalled.

findings.summary: useGenerationInvalidation.ts is a deprecated re-export shim pointing to hooks/invalidation/, but 14 files still import from the deprecated path instead of the new location.
evidence: useGenerationInvalidation.ts: `@deprecated Import from '@/shared/hooks/invalidation' instead.`

14 files still import from the deprecated path (useShotsCrud.ts, useShotCreation.ts, useLoadVariantImages.ts, useTimelineCore.ts, ShotEditorView.tsx, useVariants.ts, useShotGenerationMetadata.ts, VideoGenerationModal.tsx, useMakeMainVariant.ts, useRepositionVariantSave.ts, useDeleteActions.ts, useApplySettingsHandler.ts, useTimelinePositions.ts, useTrimSave.ts)
findings.suggestion: Update all 14 imports to use '@/shared/hooks/invalidation' and delete the shim file.
findings.reasoning: The shim has been in place but no consumer has been migrated. This creates confusion about which is the canonical import path.

findings.summary: useShots.ts is a barrel re-export from hooks/shots/ with 20+ consumers still importing from the old path. The file header says 'New code should import directly from the modular shots hook folder' but the migration hasn't happened.
evidence: useShots.ts lines 4-5: 'This file provides backwards compatibility for existing imports. New code should import directly from the modular shots hook folder.'
20+ files still import from '@/shared/hooks/useShots' including App.tsx, ShotsContext.tsx, ShotEditorView.tsx
findings.suggestion: Either complete the migration (update all imports to use hooks/shots/) or accept this as the stable public API and remove the deprecation notice. Given 20+ consumers, the barrel re-export IS the public API at this point.
findings.reasoning: With this many consumers, the barrel file is effectively permanent. The deprecation notice creates false expectations.

findings.summary: ProjectImageSettings has 6 deprecated properties that are still read by active code (selectedLorasByTextModel used in useGenerationSource.ts, selectedReferenceId used in migration paths).
evidence: Line 129: `/** @deprecated migrated to selectedLorasByCategory */ selectedLorasByTextModel`
Line 131: `/** @deprecated moved to tool settings */ projectPrompts`
Line 135: `/** @deprecated use selectedReferenceIdByShot */ selectedReferenceId`
Lines 137-146: Five more @deprecated fields for style reference
useGenerationSource.ts line 121-122: actively reads selectedLorasByTextModel as fallback
findings.suggestion: The deprecated fields that are still read as migration fallbacks should have migration TODOs with target dates. The ones that are truly unused (projectPrompts) should be removed.
findings.reasoning: Deprecated fields without a migration plan accumulate indefinitely. The fallback read in useGenerationSource.ts suggests the migration is incomplete.

findings.summary: LAST_ACTIVE_LORA_SETTINGS and GLOBAL_LAST_ACTIVE_LORA_SETTINGS are marked deprecated but still defined and tested.
evidence: Line 14: `// @deprecated - LoRAs are now part of LAST_ACTIVE_SHOT_SETTINGS`
Line 22: `// @deprecated - LoRAs are now part of GLOBAL_LAST_ACTIVE_SHOT_SETTINGS`
storageKeys.test.ts lines 15-33: Tests still validate the deprecated keys
findings.suggestion: If no code reads these keys anymore, remove them and their tests. If old localStorage entries need to be cleaned up, add a one-time migration.
findings.reasoning: Deprecated symbols that exist only for backwards compatibility with localStorage entries that may or may not exist are dead weight.

findings.summary: VariantBadge.tsx declares dismissHoverDelay as @deprecated but the prop is still in the interface. No consumers pass it.
evidence: Line 52-53: `/** @deprecated No longer used - dismiss action is in tooltip */ dismissHoverDelay?: number;`
Grep shows only the definition, no consumers
findings.suggestion: Remove the prop from the interface since nothing uses it.
findings.reasoning: Trivial cleanup - deprecated prop with zero consumers should just be deleted.

findings.summary: useSmartPolling has deeply nested conditional branching for mobile multipliers that obscures the core polling logic. The mobile/desktop branching is interleaved with the polling interval computation.
evidence: Lines 84-85: Mobile detection via user agent regex
Lines 140-143: mobileMultiplier applied to every interval
Lines 145-171: Each branch has mobile/desktop variants of pollingReason strings
Lines 196-197: Dead comment `// [MobileHeatDebug] Log aggressive polling on mobile for debugging` with no code
findings.suggestion: Apply the mobile multiplier once at the end instead of threading it through every branch. Compute `rawInterval`, then `finalInterval = rawInterval * mobileMultiplier`. This eliminates all the effectiveMaxInterval / effectiveMinInterval intermediate variables.
findings.reasoning: The mobile concern is cross-cutting but implemented inline, doubling the branch count. A single post-hoc multiplier would be cleaner.

findings.summary: createImageGenerationTask builds its params object using nested IIFEs and spread patterns that are hard to follow, especially the LoRA merging logic which has two separate IIFE blocks that construct the same lorasMap.
evidence: Lines 346-362: First IIFE builds lorasMap from params.loras + in_this_scene LoRA
Lines 394-406: Second IIFE rebuilds the same lorasMap from params.loras, then merges with additional_loras
Lines 364-382: Another IIFE for style reference filtering
findings.suggestion: Build the task params in sequential steps with named variables instead of nested IIFEs inside object spread. First compute `loras`, then `referenceParams`, then assemble the final object.
findings.reasoning: The two IIFEs that both iterate params.loras are duplicated work and the nesting makes the data flow hard to trace. Sequential named steps would be more readable.

findings.summary: calculateTaskResolution has a legacy overload signature that accepts positional args (projectId, customResolution, modelName) alongside the new options-object form, adding complexity to handle both.
evidence: Lines 234-236: Options-object signature
Lines 241-245: `@deprecated` positional-args signature
Lines 247-255: Implementation dispatches between the two forms
Line 305-308: The only callsite using the old API is createImageGenerationTask (internal function)
findings.suggestion: Since calculateTaskResolution is not exported (internal function), update createImageGenerationTask to use the options form and remove the deprecated overload.
findings.reasoning: The deprecated overload adds complexity to an internal-only function. Its only caller is in the same file.

findings.summary: LoRA validation logic (path required, strength 0-2) is duplicated between validateImageGenerationParams and validateBatchImageGenerationParams.
evidence: Lines 160-169: LoRA validation in validateImageGenerationParams
Lines 195-205: Identical LoRA validation in validateBatchImageGenerationParams
Line 194: Comment explicitly acknowledges duplication: `// Validate loras if provided (same as single image)`
findings.suggestion: Extract a `validateLoras(loras)` helper and call it from both functions.
findings.reasoning: The comment even acknowledges the duplication. A shared helper would be trivial and prevent the two copies from drifting.

findings.summary: debugPolling.ts error logging destructures error objects into verbose multi-field objects that don't add value over just logging the error directly.
evidence: Lines 24-30: `console.error('[PollingDebug] Connection test failed:', { error, errorMessage: error.message, errorCode: error.code, errorDetails: error.details, errorHint: error.hint })`
Lines 57-63: Same verbose destructuring pattern for query test
findings.suggestion: Just log `console.error('[PollingDebug] Connection test failed:', error)`. The error object already contains all these fields and browser devtools display them.
findings.reasoning: Manually spreading an error's fields into a new object is redundant - the original error is more useful for debugging since it preserves the stack trace.

findings.summary: VariantBadge has two nearly-identical JSX blocks for overlay and inline variants that differ only in wrapper className and badge size prop.
evidence: Lines 95-136: Overlay variant JSX
Lines 139-171: Inline variant JSX - same StatusBadge + TouchableTooltip structure with minor class differences
findings.suggestion: Extract the badge content (StatusBadge + TouchableTooltip div) into a local variable and wrap it differently for overlay vs inline. Or use a single render with conditional wrapper classes.
findings.reasoning: The two branches have identical logic flow with only className differences. Changes to badge behavior would need to be made in two places.
